title: Yeast Infection Tests: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:

Learn more about laboratory tests, reference ranges, and understanding results .
Is there anything else I need to know about a yeast test? 
You may have a higher risk of a yeast infection if you:
Are taking antibiotics . Certain antibiotics can cause yeast to grow out of control. So, be sure to tell your provider about any medicines you are taking. 
Are pregnant . Hormonal changes may increase your risk of yeast infections. 
Have HIV . People with HIV have a higher risk of esophageal candidiasis. 
At-home tests for vaginal yeast infections can tell you if your symptoms are likely to be from a yeast infection. But they may not be able to confirm the diagnosis. It's best to talk with your provider about your symptoms and which test is best for you. This is important, because many symptoms of a vaginal yeast infection are the same as the symptoms of certain sexually transmitted infections Â (STIs).
